project_name: MPS Auth - School IT Admin Portal

theme_and_archetype.reasoning: This is a centralized IT authentication and user management portal for school administrators. It must project authority, security, and absolute clarity. The design relies on high-contrast monochromes, crisp grid structures, and signal colors for functionality, eliminating unnecessary decorative elements to focus on dense data correlation and operational speed.

typography.strategy: The 'Corporate/Enterprise' pairing. Authoritative geometric sans for headings, highly legible grotesque sans for body, and strict monospaced fonts for technical data.

headings.usage: Use for Page Titles, Modal Headers, and primary metrics.

body.usage: Use for all standard UI text, form labels, and general reading.

data.usage: CRITICAL: Use for all tabular data, Audit Log entries, JWT references, user IDs, dates, and metric numbers.

colors.philosophy: Color is function only. 95% of the UI should be pure monochrome. Signal Blue indicates primary action; Signal Red indicates destructive action or critical logs. The 'Tinted Grey' rule applies: greys must have a slight cool (blue) tint to feel professional, not dead.

spacing_and_layout.philosophy: The 'Control Room' Grid (Mode B). Dense, Data Correlation, Scanability. Every pixel is utilized. No jagged bottoms on grid layouts.

alignment.tables: Left-align all text, right-align numbers and actions.
alignment.forms: Strict left-alignment for labels and inputs. Stacked layout for better scanability.

surfaces_and_components.surface_strategy: Grid Borders (The 'Technical' Look). Avoid floating soft-shadow cards. Use strict, flat borders to expose the skeleton of the UI.
surfaces_and_components.borders_and_radius: Almost brutalist. Use `rounded-sm` (4px) maximum for inputs and buttons. Widgets should have sharp 1px solid borders (`border-border`). No soft shadows.

shadcn_customizations.Button: Remove default shadow. Use `rounded-sm`. Hover states must be solid color shifts, no opacity fades.
shadcn_customizations.Input: Use `rounded-sm`. Focus state MUST use `focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-primary focus-visible:ring-offset-2`.
shadcn_customizations.Card: Strip away all drop shadows (`shadow-none`). Keep only `border border-border`. Background pure white.
shadcn_customizations.Table: Use `border-b` for rows. Hover states for rows should use `hover:bg-muted/50`. Table header must use the 'labels' typography scale (uppercase, tracking).
shadcn_customizations.Badge: Use square or slightly rounded badges. High contrast text.
shadcn_customizations.Tabs: Override default Shadcn background. Use a minimal bottom-border style for tab triggers rather than encapsulated pills to look more enterprise.

icons.style: Use regular or bold weights for high legibility in dense tables. Do not use duotone for admin portals.

navigation_and_header.style: Solid background with a hard bottom border (`border-b border-border`). Background pure white.
navigation_and_header.layout: Left: MPS Auth text logo (Chivo Black, text-xl). Center/Left: Main Navigation Links. Right: Admin Profile & Logout CTA.
navigation_and_header.links: Must be visible on desktop (no hamburger menu unless on mobile viewport). Active states should have a bold font-weight and a primary-colored bottom border indicator.

motion_and_interactions.philosophy: Minimalist — use micro-interactions only for immediate feedback.
motion_and_interactions.feedback: Use Sonner for toast notifications on all CRUD actions (User created, App assigned, Token revoked).

accessibility_and_testing.contrast: Strict WCAG AA minimum. All text over backgrounds must exceed 4.5:1 ratio.
accessibility_and_testing.focus: Visible focus rings are MANDATORY for all inputs, buttons, and links.
accessibility_and_testing.testing_attributes: CRITICAL: Every interactive element MUST have a `data-testid` attribute in kebab-case. Examples: `data-testid="login-email-input"`, `data-testid="submit-login-button"`, `data-testid="create-user-modal-trigger"`, `data-testid="revoke-session-button"`.

page_specific_instructions.auth_login: Two-column layout on desktop. Left side: High-contrast login form centered in a generous container. Right side: Edge-to-edge abstract architectural image (from image_urls) covered with a subtle `bg-black/10` overlay to add depth without obscuring. Use `data-testid` for email, password, and submit.
page_specific_instructions.auth_onboarding: Wizard flow. Step-by-step centered card layout (`max-w-2xl`). Progress indicator at the top. Use clean transition animations between steps.
page_specific_instructions.auth_dashboard: The 'Control Room'. Top row: 3-4 Key Metric cards (Total Users, Active Sessions, Apps, System Health). Below: A split view—Left (col-span-8): Recent Audit Log table. Right (col-span-4): Quick Actions or System Alerts.
page_specific_instructions.auth_users: Dense data table. Left aligned. Search bar and filter dropdowns (Role, App) explicitly visible above the table. Action menu (three dots) on the far right of each row for Edit/Deactivate.
page_specific_instructions.auth_audit: Pure technical view. Full-width table. Use `IBM Plex Mono` for timestamps, IP addresses, and User IDs. Distinct badge colors for Actions (e.g., green for Login, red for Failed Login, blue for Permission Change).

login_hero.description: Monochrome abstract architectural structure with triangular geometric patterns. Perfect for the right side of the split login screen, projecting structure, system, and enterprise scale.
login_hero.placement_instructions: Use as `object-cover h-full w-full` on the right half of the `/auth/login` page on desktop breakpoints. Add a very light overlay if needed for contrast.

onboarding_background.description: Low angle shot of a modern university building facade. Connects the software to the physical 'school' context professionally.
onboarding_background.placement_instructions: Use as a subtle blurred background or side panel during the first-time admin setup wizard.

system_health_illustration.description: Detailed view of network server racks in a modern data center.
system_health_illustration.placement_instructions: Can be used as an avatar, empty state illustration, or banner in the Apps/System management sections.
